rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC. VS. ERNEST MASON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Correct Mistake In Memorandum Of Costs, Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SACOR FINANCIAL, INC.'S Motion To Correct Mistake In Memorandum Of Costs. Assignee of record Saccor Financial, Inc.'s motion to correct mistake in memorandum of costs is denied. CCP 473(b) which requires that the motion be made within six months, not CCP 473(a) which does not carry a time limit, is the applicable code section permitting relief for a party from its own mistake. Since this motion was filed more than six months after the asserted mistake, the motion is untimely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Saccor is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. SUNNATILLO MUHAMADIEV ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's unopposed motion to compel responses to form interrogatories is granted. Defendant Muhamadiev shall serve code-compliant responses to the form interrogatories no later than January 24, 2019. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) ET AL VS. DIMITRIOU &ASSOCIATES, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANTS DIMITRIOU & ASSOC. P.C., ANDREW DIMITRIOU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendants Andrew Dimitriou and Dimitriou & Assoc. P.C.'s motion to set aside the defaults entered against them on March 21, 2018 is granted and both defaults are now vacated. The sole basis for the opposition to this motion is that the motion is untimely since it was filed more than six months after the defaults were entered. Liberally construing the motion papers, one of the statutory bases for the motion is the mandatory "attorney fault" provision of CCP 473(b) based on the declarations of Andrew Dimitriou and Kevin Sullivan. The six month deadline for obtaining relief per that provision runs from the entry of a default judgment and thus this motion is timely filed since no default judgment has been entered. (Sugasawara v. Newland (1994) 27 Cal. App. 4th 294). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOYTR, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. TINYCO, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jam City, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 12, Defendant Jam City, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant Jam City, Inc's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. As an initial matter, the determination of the amount owed by TinyCo per the Amended and Restated Platform Agreement is not final (and thus not subject to claim preclusion) due to plaintiff Loytr, Inc.'s filing of an appeal in case number 551519. Even if the determination of the amount owed by TinyCo to Loytr was final and amenable to claim preclusion, on the record on this motion there are triable disputes: a) whether the parties to case number 551519 actually litigated the issue of the amount, if any, owed by Jam City to Loytr for revenue Jam City received for TinyCo games covered by the Agreement; and b) if the parties to case number 551519 did not actually litigate that issue, whether Jam City waived its right to assert the defense of claim preclusion due to its conduct in case number 551519 and this case. Loytr's claims against Jam City are based on the same primary right as its claims against TinyCo adjudicated in 551519 because in both actions Loytr seeks to recover monies alleged owed to it under the Agreement. It is far from clear based on the record on this motion whether the issue of the amount, if any, owed by Jam City to Loytr for revenue Jam City received for TinyCo games covered by the Agreement was actually litigated in case number 551519 due to the parties' failure to provide definitive evidence of what actually occurred at the trial in that case and the parties' disagreements about what actually occurred at the trial. Significantly, as the party who bears the initial burden on all issues on this motion, Jam City did not submit any evidence in its moving papers whether the issue of Jam City's receipt of any revenue from TinyCo Games (apart from the revenue received by TinyCo that was actually litigated) that was subject to revenue share under the Agreement was actually litigated. Per United Bank & Trust Co. of California v. Hunt (1934) 1 Cal.2d 340, there is a triable issue whether Jam City waived its right to object to the splitting of claims based on among, other things, the parties' stipulation regarding the motion to consolidate the two cases. The penultimate sentence in Gamble v. General Foods Corporation (1991) 229 Cal. App. 3d 893 distinguishing United Bank does not require granting this motion because Loyter's waiver argument is not based solely or even mostly on the denial of its motion to amend in case 551519. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Loytr is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF SAN ANTONIO SHOE INC's Motion For An Order Terminating Sanctions Or Additional Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for issue sanctions against Defendant James Barchard is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant Barchard has failed to comply with this Court's order compelling him to respond to Plaintiff's discovery requests. Defendant has not filed any papers opposing Plaintiff's motion or explaining his failure to comply with his discovery obligations and this Court's order. Accordingly, pursuant to CCP Section 2030.030(c), this Court issues an issue sanctions against Defendant James Barchard, which prohibits him from introducing into evidence any documents, testimony, or other evidence that would have been responsive to any of Plaintiff's discovery requests. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. rulings.Case Title: FATUMA WANG VS. RAYMOND E. BOLAND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT RAYMOND BOLAND'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint. Defendant Raymond Boland's motion to strike the punitive damages portions of the complaint filed by plaintiff Fatuma Wang is denied. The allegations that Mr. Boland fled the scene of the accident "when he knew that he had driven his vehicle into ¿. Leila J. [and] ¿ observed her laying in the crosswalk injured and in pain" suffices to allege malice as that word is defined in Civil Code 3294(c)(1). Mr. Boland's argument that his alleged fleeing of the scene was not an "injury-producing event" is neither established by the language of the complaint nor supported by common sense and experience. Nothing in the complaint states or suggests that the request for general damages is not applicable to Mr. Boland's alleged fleeing of the scene. Moreover, it is entirely possible, if not probable, that an injured person would suffer additional pain and suffering by seeing that the party who injured her failed to offer any assistance to her and tried to avoid being identified. The court brings to the parties' attention that, notwithstanding that an order was filed on December 12, 2018 continuing the hearing on this motion to January 14, 2019, on December 13, 2018 the court erroneously entered an order granting the motion. The December 13, 2018 order is now vacated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Wang is required to prepare a proposed order which is entitled "Order Vacating the December 13, 2018 Order Granting Defendant's Motion to Strike the Punitive Damages Allegations and Entering a New and Different Order Denying the Motion" and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE LONGMORE VS. NORTH STAR DEVELOPMENT HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT NORTH STAR DEVELOPMENT HOLDINGS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant North Star Development Holdings LLC's demurrer to the second and fourth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Wayne Longmore is overruled as to both causes of action. As to the second cause of action, Mr. Longmore alleges sufficient facts showing that the parties waived the written modification requirement. Paragraphs 11 and 42 of the first amended complaint allege that North Star modified the agreement by reducing the base salary without a writing. "When one party has, through oral representations and conduct or custom, subsequently behaved in a manner antithetical to one or more terms of an express written contract, he or she has induced the other party to rely on the representations and conduct or custom. In that circumstance, it would be equally inequitable to deny the relying party the benefit of the other party's apparent modification of the written contract." (Wagner v. Glendale Adventist Med. Ctr. (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1379, 1388.) In addition, Mr. Longmore alleges that when the parties signed the UWI Mona Agreement, North Star's president (Mr. Tippett) told Mr. Longmore that he would now be a rich man and implied that Mr. Longmore would receive his economic interest. "The rule is well settled that in construing the terms of a contract the construction given it by the acts and conduct of the parties with knowledge of its terms, and before any controversy has arisen as to its meaning, is admissible on the issue of the parties' intent." (Southern Cal. Edison Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 839, 851.) As to the fourth cause of action, liberally reading the first amended complaint as required per CCP 452, Mr. Longmore alleges sufficient facts showing that the implied contract is outside the scope of the express contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Longmore is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY GALUSKY VS. NINA ASHLEY VANESS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Timothy Galusky To Provide Further Responses At His Continued Deposition And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Statement Of Items; Declaration Of Erika K. Bornstein In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT NINA VANESS' Motion To Compel Plaintiff Timothy Galusky To Provide Further Responses At His Continued Deposition And For Sanctions. Defendant Nina Vaness' motion to compel plaintiff Timothy Galusky to provide further responses at his continued deposition and for monetary sanctions is denied and Mr. Galusky's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. None of the six sets of deposition questions identified in Ms. Vaness'statement of items in dispute seek sufficiently probative information to override Mr. Galusky's medical privacy privilege. While it is possible that information about other portions of Mr. Galusky's body than those portions allegedly injured in the accident that is the subject of this case may be discoverable on the issue of general damages, Ms. Vaness has offered no facts to warrant requiring Mr. Galusky to answer the questions he declined to answer at his deposition. Although it is a close call, the court finds that there was substantial justification for the motion. Ms. Vaness' counsel is admonished that she was not given leave to file any further papers after the hearing before the Judge Pro Tem and should not have filed any further papers without receiving leave to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gallusky is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 14, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT PHOTOWORKS, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Photoworks, Inc.'s demurrer to all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Karl Reeves is overruled as to all causes of action. Based on the showing made by Mr. Reeves in the opposition papers and per Rojas v. Cutsworth (1998) 67 Cal. App. 4th 774, the complaint is deemed filed on October 23, 2018, which establishes that all causes of action were filed within a year of Mr. Reeves' receipt of a right to sue letter.
